single_concern_assessment: v5's per-AC dependency demonstration substantively justifies bundling for 7 of 8 ACs (AC-1/2/3/4/5/6/7) via concrete circular or one-way dependencies grounded in mechanism (e.g. AC-1 row literally invokes ESLint on staged diff, consuming AC-3 config; lcov-parser consumes AC-6 vitest config). AC-8 'documentation-system invariant' is the weakest leg — the divergence-window argument is real but soft, since AC-8 is a CLAUDE.md stub that could plausibly land in a same-day follow-up PR. However the cost of splitting AC-8 alone exceeds the benefit, and the rationale is now concrete rather than rhetorical.

v4_finding_response_audit.note: AC-1 mechanism extended to invoke ESLint; per-AC dependency table replaces handwave with concrete deps.
7 clauses (a)-(g) cover timing, entropy, fallback, meta-tests, log-leakage; heredoc substitution safe (no re-expansion of {NONCE} literals).
Table uses <this-v5-commit> placeholder + footnote; placeholder cannot resolve until commit lands (chicken-and-egg, but footnote covers).
Caveat in S-37-5 row + End-of-session-37 line; not in DoD but DoD describes end-state correctly.

findings.description: AC-8 dependency rationale (line 15: 'documentation-system invariant') is the weakest of the 5 bundling justifications. The argument 'shipping AC-8 separately creates a window where docs document phantom gates or gates are undocumented' is real but the window could be ≤1 hour if AC-8 ships as a follow-up commit on the same PR. A purist could still argue AC-8 belongs in v3a-ii. Cost-of-split now plausibly exceeds benefit so this is nit-not-RC, but rationale could be sharper by noting that AC-8's stub is referenced from AC-7's verdict-vocabulary spec (line 53 cites G23 vocabulary documented inline in AC-8) — that's a real circular link not currently surfaced in the bundle table.
findings.recommendation: Optional v6 polish: add to bundle table line 15 'AC-8 also hosts the verdict vocabulary (approve/nit-only/request-changes/block) that AC-7's plan-review subagent references — splitting AC-8 leaves AC-7 citing an undefined vocabulary'. Not blocking; address as PR-comment follow-up if at all.

findings.description: STATUS line (line 3) claims v5 closes I3 with '§15 table extended with 8a79752 + 5d9c069 + open-ended footnote'. The actual table at lines 26-36 uses placeholder `<this-v5-commit>` rather than literal SHA (the v5-revision SHA is unknowable until the commit is created — git chicken-and-egg). The STATUS narrative slightly overstates: 5d9c069 IS literally listed; 8a79752 IS literally listed; the v5-revision commit itself is the placeholder. Cosmetic mismatch between status-narrative and table content; the I3 closure footnote (line 38) does cover this honestly.
findings.recommendation: Optional: amend STATUS line to '§15 table extended with 8a79752 + 5d9c069 + placeholder-for-this-commit + open-ended footnote' for verbatim accuracy. Trivial; PR-comment follow-up acceptable.

summary: v5 is convergent and ships. Trajectory v3=8 → v4=4 → v5=2-nit confirms the iteration loop is finding diminishing returns. I1's AC-3↔AC-1 dependency is now structurally real (AC-1 row literally invokes ESLint); I2's 7-clause nonce hardening is defensible (heredoc substitution is safe because already-expanded PLAN_CONTENT contains no further $-expansion); I3 placeholder + footnote is acceptable given git's chicken-and-egg constraint; I4 caveat appears in both S-37-5 and End-of-session-37 lines. Two remaining nits are sharpenings rather than substantive issues — AC-8 bundle rationale could surface its real link to AC-7's verdict vocabulary, and STATUS narrative slightly over-states the §15 SHA list. Recommended verdict: nit-only — ship + open follow-up issue for J1+J2 if desired. Proceed to AC-1 impl per H6 sub-sequence (S-37-1 failing-meta-tests RED commit first).
